Title: Facility directory disclosure process established
Description: Establish and maintain a documented process for including protected health information (PHI) in facility directories, ensuring individuals are informed and given the opportunity to agree or object to such disclosures.
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Facility directory policy documents detailing the inclusion process and notification procedures.- Records of individuals’ directory preferences, including opt-out or restriction requests.- Documentation of disclosures made from the directory, including the circumstances and recipients.

Title: Full card numbers protected and hidden
Description: Implement different measures to protect full card numbers (i.e. PANs) such as hiding most of the number on screens and receipts with something like asterisks (\*\*\*\*\*\*\*). Establish a list of job roles who need full access, including a rationale why this is the case.Set up your payment system so that it only shows full numbers to people on your access list. Be extra careful with remote access and set up controls to only let those with a real business need copy card numbers.Train your staff accordingly. Review access rights to full card numbers regularly and check your system configurations.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Screenshots showing masked card numbers on your system- List of roles that need full card number access, with justification- Settings or logs from your remote access tools showing copy restrictions- Records of staff training about protecting card numbers

Title: Honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) Signals
Description: Detect and honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals from users’ browsers or devices in accordance with CCPA requirements. It automatically respects users’ preferences to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. The control also logs and monitors signal processing to maintain compliance and provide evidence of adherence to consumer privacy requests.
Instructions: ### Action itemsDesign and implement a GPC management system that detects when a user has these settings configured on their device or browser, and automatically respects the user's preferences. Capture logs to demonstrate that this process is successful.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Logs showing successful signals being captured, processed, and respected.

Title: Information backup
Description: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

Title: Information deletion
Description: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

Title: Information transfer
Description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties.

Title: Input validation used
Description: Implement input validation checks across all system interfaces to ensure data completeness and accuracy before processing. These checks should verify that data meets specified format requirements and business rules.
Instructions: #### Action ItemsSet up automated input validation for all data entry points. This includes checking data types, required fields, and format requirements. Document all validation rules and ensure they are consistently applied across the system.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Screenshots of input validation code implementations- Documentation of validation rules and requirements- Error logs showing rejected invalid inputs- Test results demonstrating validation effectiveness

Title: Key management process implemented
Description: Implement a key management system covering the entire lifecycle of cryptographic keys.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsDevelop procedures for key generation, distribution, storage, use, rotation, and destruction. Include processes for handling compromised keys, key recovery, and responding to legal requests for key access.Establish a secure key storage system with role-based access. Implement logging and auditing for all key management activities. Train relevant staff on key management practices and security implications.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Documented key management and encryption processes defining generation, rotation, and revocation.2. Key management logs showing dates of generation, rotation, and revocation.3. Documentation of regular key inventory reviews, including dates and responsible personnel.

Title: Labelling of information
Description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organization.

Title: Maintain Record of Processing Activities
Description: The organization must maintain a comprehensive and regularly updated record of all personal information processing activities to demonstrate compliance with CCPA.
Instructions: ### Action itemsCreate and commit to maintaining a log of processing activities including at minimum the following details: a. Categories of personal information processed, collected, or shared (PI [Big Bird, Male, frequents nestadornaments.com, recently purchased Stick Bundle Variety Pack] / SPI [Bigus Canarius (Race/heritage), Puppet (job), 4049 0000 0000 0000 (card number)]).    b. Purposes of collecting information.    c. Categories of sources from where this information was obtained.     d. categories of 3rd parties this information is shared with.  e. retention periods and deletion criteria for each category of information.   f. security and privacy controls defending the information.  g. identification of the data owner.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. A spreadsheet or other log including all of the action items

Title: Media accounted for and documented during transportation
Description: Establish a process to protect and control system media containing CUI during transport outside of controlled areas. Ensure accountability through documented activities and tracking of media to prevent loss, tampering, or destruction.
Instructions: ### Action items- Create a process to log and document when media containing CUI is transported outside of controlled areas, where physical or procedural measures are in place to safeguard CUI.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- A link or screenshot of past transport logs and documented activities.

Title: Media physically secured
Description: Implement physical security measures such as storing all media (like paper documents, laptops, USB drives) in locked cabinets and restricting access to only authorized personnel. Develop a system to classify all media based on the sensitivity of the data it contains. Maintain an inventory for all media, both electronic and physical, and conduct a full inventory check at least once every 12 months.Establish procedures for secure distribution (e.g., logging of all media sent outside the facility, using secure couriers). Obtain management approval before moving any media offsite.Create and log procedures for secure destruction (e.g., shredding paper, secure data wiping methods).Train employees at least yearly so that they can identify and manage this type of confidential data.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Photographs of locked storage areas and shredding equipment- Copies of employee training materials- Completed logs of media transfers, access to secure areas, and media destruction- Screenshots or exports of the electronic media inventory spreadsheet

Title: Notice of privacy practices created and distributed
Description: Distribute a Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP) to patients, informing them of their rights under HIPAA and how their PHI will be used and disclosed.
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Copies of the NPP provided to patients.- Acknowledgment forms signed by patients confirming receipt of the NPP.- Records of periodic reviews and updates to the NPP.

Title: PHI access and amendment requests managed
Description: Establish and maintain a documented process to manage individuals' requests for accessing and amending their protected health information (PHI).
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Logs of PHI access and amendment requests, including dates, outcomes, and any justifications for denial.- Copies of written responses to amendment requests, including acceptance or denial letters.- Records of PHI provided to individuals in the requested format or justification for alternative formats.

Title: PHI disclosure records maintained
Description: Establish a system to track disclosures of PHI, ensuring records of disclosures meet HIPAA requirements for accountability and transparency.
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Logs of all PHI disclosures with the required details.- Disclosure tracking reports reviewed periodically by compliance personnel.- Documentation of procedures for responding to requests for accounting of disclosures.

Title: Patient authorization for PHI use and disclosure obtained
Description: Require and maintain patient authorizations for the use or disclosure of PHI outside of permitted purposes (treatment, payment, and healthcare operations).
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Completed authorization forms signed by patients.- Records of training for staff on the use and management of authorization forms.- Audit logs verifying that disclosures without authorization are limited to permitted purposes

Title: Privacy and protection of PII
Description: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII according to applicable laws and regulations and contractual requirements.

Title: Privacy disclosure and notification mechanisms established
Description: Implement and maintain active communication channels for informing individuals about specific instances of personal data collection, use, and disclosure. The goal is to provide timely, context-specific privacy information beyond what's in the general privacy policy.Develop templates for data sharing, policy changes, and breach notifications so they can be sent to the relevant stakeholders when needed. This includes identifying relevant authorities, setting up systems to deliver these notices via appropriate channels (e.g., app, email), and establishing a process to handle responses to these notifications.Regularly review and update these mechanisms.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Screenshots of in-app or point-of-collection privacy disclosures- Logs of sent notifications (e.g., emails about privacy practice changes)- Templates for various types of privacy notifications (e.g., breaches)- List of relevant authorities, including their contact details, that need to be notified in case of information security incidents

Title: Privacy policy created and maintained
Description: A privacy policy is developed, regularly updated, and made accessible to all relevant parties. The goal is to enable transparent communication of data handling practices and to protect individuals' privacy rights.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Current version of the privacy policy with timestamp- Documentation of annual policy reviews and updates- Screenshots showing the privacy policy's accessibility on the company website- Training records demonstrating employee awareness of the privacy policy

Title: Processing integrity policy established
Description: Establish systematic controls in your processing integrity policy that ensure the completeness, accuracy, validity, timeliness, and authorization of system processing while maintaining data quality throughout the entire processing lifecycle.
Instructions: Document a processing integrity policy covering data quality specifications, input validation requirements, processing controls, output verification, and storage integrity. Define processes for data validation, error handling, monitoring, and quality assurance. Establish frameworks for measuring and maintaining processing accuracy and completeness. Create procedures for managing processing changes, capacity monitoring, and system performance.Document requirements for quality metrics, processing logs, error tracking, and regular processing reviews.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Creating a processing integrity policy in the Oneleet policy builder.- A PDF upload of your processing integrity policy hosted in your documents provider.- Screenshots showing implementation of your data validation processes and quality controls.- System logs demonstrating input validation, processing monitoring, and output verification.- Documentation of error handling procedures and correction logs.

Title: Record copies generated and export enabled
Description: Enable generating complete and precise copies of electronic records in both human‑readable and electronic formats for regulatory examination.
Instructions: #### Action Items1. Implement export/download functionality (PDF, CSV, XML).2. Enable human‑readable display or print views.3. Validate fidelity of outputs.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Sample exports (PDF/CSV) showing full record including audit trails and e‑signatures.- UI screenshots of export workflows.- Fidelity comparison reports between original and exported records.

Title: Right to Delete Request Handling
Description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-delete management. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their data deleted upon request.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsEstablish 2 methods of contact for a user to express a right-to-delete request. (web form, phone, email)### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Screenshot of privacy policy section that includes methods of delete request handling, Screenshot of backend showing the process for handling. 2. Logs showing the process had been successfully completed for a current user.

Title: Right to Limit Use of Sensitive PI
Description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-limit use of SPI. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their sensitive data not be shared upon request.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsEstablish 2 methods of contact for a user to express a right to limit use of SPI-request. (web form, phone, email)### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Screenshot of privacy policy section that includes methods of right to limit use request handling, Screenshot of backend showing the process for handling. 2. Logs showing the process had been successfully completed for a current user.

Title: Right to Opt out of Sale and Sharing
Description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-Opt-out of sale. Under CCPA, the user has a right to have their data not be shared upon request.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsEstablish 2 methods of contact for a user to express a right-to-opt-out-of-sale request. (web form, phone, email)### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Screenshot of privacy policy section that includes methods of opt out request handling, Screenshot of backend showing the process for handling. 2. Logs showing the process had been successfully completed for a current user.

Title: Right-to-Know Request Handling
Description: The organization must configure a system to handle Right-to-know management. Under CCPA, the user has a right to know what data of theirs has been collected.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsEstablish 2 methods of contact for a user to express a right-to-know request. (web form, phone, email)### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Screenshot of privacy policy section that includes methods of right-to-know handling. Screenshot of backend showing the process for handling.2. Logs showing the process had been successfully completed for a current user.

Title: Role-Specific Privacy Training
Description: This control ensures that all employees receive role-specific privacy training to understand their responsibilities under the CCPA and related privacy laws. The training covers the handling of personal information, consumer rights, and organizational policies for protecting data. Completion is tracked, and refresher sessions are provided periodically to maintain awareness and compliance across the workforce.
Instructions: ### Action itemsAssign role-based security training by user or by group. Ensure that everyone takes a CCPA training.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Oneleet monitors for CCPA training passing 2. Uploaded records of course completion for the entire workforce, including the name and scope of the course they took.

Title: Secure disposal process established
Description: Establish and maintain a documented procedure for the secure disposal of electronic and physical media containing protected or classified data. This process renders the data unusable, unreadable, and indecipherable to prevent unauthorized access.
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- A documented disposal procedure describing methods for securely destroying media containing protected information- Logs or records of disposal activities, including media type, date, method of destruction, and verification.- Certificates of destruction provided by third-party vendors for media containing protected information.- Incident records demonstrating how the disposal procedure was followed during media destruction events.

Title: Sensitive payment information automatically deleted
Description: Implement a system that automatically deletes sensitive payment information after it's no longer needed for business or legal purposes.For the purposes of this control sensitive data is any of the following:- Full track data (all the info from a card's magnetic stripe)- Card verification code (the 3 or 4 digit code on the card)- PIN (Personal Identification Number) or PIN blockConfigure your payment system to automatically delete this sensitive data right after approval. If you must keep it temporarily and have a solid business justification, make sure it's encrypted and deleted as soon as possible.Implement regular checks and reviews to make sure that no sensitive data is being stored. Train your team and instruct them to not write down or store this sensitive data
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Screenshots of your payment system configuration showing your payment system doesn't store this data- Logs showing automatic deletion of sensitive data after transactions- Records of staff training on this topic

Title: Signature manifestation on human‑readable forms displayed
Description: Ensure that the printed name, date/time of signing, and meaning of the signature appear and are controlled like the record itself on any display or printout of a signed electronic record.
Instructions: #### Action Items1. Configure UI and print/PDF templates to display: signer’s printed name, signing date/time (with time zone), and meaning (e.g., Approved/Reviewed).2. Ensure these fields are non‑editable and part of the record rendering.3. Validate that displays/printouts include manifestations for all signed states.4. Time‑synchronize systems used to render signatures.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Screenshots and PDFs of signed records showing name, timestamp, and meaning.- Template or report definitions demonstrating non‑editable signature blocks.- Test scripts/results verifying manifestations appear on screen and printouts.- NTP configuration records/time sync logs.

Title: Signature manifestation provided
Description: Ensure electronic signatures clearly indicate signer identity, timestamp, and purpose on human‑readable outputs.
Instructions: #### Action Items1. Display printed name, timestamp, purpose on UI and print.2. Include same in exports.3. Apply same controls as records.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Example signed record displaying signature name, date/time, meaning.- UI configuration showing signature manifestations.- Export demonstrating proper signature metadata.

Title: Signatures bound to records
Description: Permanently link each signature to its record to prevent dissociation or fraud.
Instructions: #### Action Items1. Store signature metadata tied to record.2. Implement technical protections against detached signatures.3. Monitor tampering attempts.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- System schema showing signature metadata linkage.- Sample record with embedded signature metadata.- Audit logs showing attempted signature tampering.

Title: System use notification mechanisms established
Description: Implement and maintain a system use notification message that includes privacy and security notices and must be displayed and acknowledged before granting access to any system accessing sensitive data.
Instructions: ### Action Items- Configure a system use notification banner/message that is displayed at login before access is granted.- Ensure the notification includes privacy and security notices.- Require users to acknowledge the notification before proceeding with system access.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Screenshots of the system use notification banner/message displayed at login.- Documentation showing the approved notification text that includes privacy and security notices.- Logs showing user acknowledgment of the notification before system access.

Title: Test information
Description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

Title: Track Gaps and Remediation
Description: This control ensures that any gaps identified in the privacy program—whether through audits, assessments, or regulatory reviews—are formally tracked and addressed. It requires the organization to document each gap, assign remediation actions, monitor progress, and verify completion to maintain compliance with CCPA and related privacy requirements. The control supports continuous improvement by ensuring that privacy risks are systematically mitigated and accountability is maintained.
Instructions: ## Action itemsImplement a tracker to note any privacy or security gaps, and track resolution. ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Show the tracker designed to catch this information

Title: Travel to high-risk locations secured
Description: Establish more stringent configurations for systems or components issued to employees traveling to high-risk areas. Upon return, conduct post-travel inspections of devices.
Instructions: ### Action itemsEnsure devices have the following configurations for individuals traveling to high-risk locations:- Full-disk encryption.- Minimal software installations.- Remote wipe capabilities.- Restricted access permissions.Perform a physical inspection of returned devices to check for tampering. Conduct a malware scan to detect and remove potential threats.

Title: Unintended PHI contained and deleted
Description: Establish a process to contain and securely delete any Personal Health Information (PHI) added accidentally by your users.
Instructions: ### Action itemsDefine a clear workflow for handling unintended PHI in your systems once identified. At a minimum, this should include:- Limiting access to the identified data immediately upon detection, and- Securely deleting PHI using approved deletion or purge processes (aligned with your data management policy).Once completed, document your actions taken, including timeframes and involved personnel.### Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Internal documentation outlining your deletion/remediation process once PHI is identified- Screenshots or logs from ticketing systems or internal tools (with redacted PHI)- Secure deletion confirmation or audit logs

Title: Unintended PHI detected
Description: Implement mechanisms to detect PHI in your systems in case it accidentally gets added by your users, with an escalation process when potential PHI is detected.
Instructions: ### Action itemsSet up keyword or pattern-based alerts (e.g., using DLP tools, regex, or logging filters) to flag potential PHI in inputs such as support tickets, file uploads, or user-submitted forms.Establish a clear internal reporting and escalation workflow when potential PHI is detected.### Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Screenshot or configuration of keyword/PATTERN detection rules (e.g., regex for SSNs, ICD codes, etc.)- Alerts or logs showing flagged incidents (redacted if necessary)- Internal documentation for PHI detection and escalation- Example communication (e.g., Slack/email) showing internal reporting of a flagged PHI case

Title: Unintended PHI exposure reviewed
Description: Establish a regular process to review all instances of unintended Personal Health Information (PHI) exposure to understand the root cause, assess impact, and determine whether any changes are needed to prevent future exposures.
Instructions: ### Action itemsPerform a timely review of each PHI exposure incident once containment and deletion are complete, at a minimum including:- Identifying the source of the exposure (e.g., user behavior, product flaw, process gap).- Documenting findings and implementing lessons learned.If recurring patterns are found, consider updating your privacy risk assessments or product safeguards.### Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Post-incident review of unintended PHI exposure- Meeting notes or summaries from internal incident review- Screenshots showing revisions made to forms, UI, alerts, or documentation if applicable

Title: Use and disclosure of PHI managed
Description: Implement procedures to operationalize the policy defining the permissible uses and disclosures of protected health information (PHI), ensuring compliance with HIPAA requirements in day-to-day activities.
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- Documentation of approval workflows for non-routine disclosures.- Standard operating procedures (SOPs) detailing step-by-step processes for evaluating, approving, and documenting PHI disclosures.

Title: Verification of Consumer Identity
Description: Verify the identity of consumers submitting privacy requests in accordance with CCPA requirements. Verification procedures ensure that access, deletion, or opt-out requests are processed only for legitimate requestors, protecting personal information from unauthorized disclosure. The process is documented, monitored, and updated as needed to maintain security and regulatory compliance.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsEstablish a process for verifying the identity of the requestor to ensure that the individual owns the data being changed or accessed.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Show a written process for verifying identity of requestor2. Show a completed verification

Title: Verification procedures established
Description: Establish and maintain documented procedures for verifying the identity and authority of individuals or entities requesting access to protected information.
Instructions: Examples of evidence that fulfills this control- A written verification procedure document specifying acceptable identification and authority verification methods.- Logs of verification activities, including requestor details and methods used.- Documentation of denied requests, including reasons and any follow-up actions taken.

Title: Workflow sequencing and process controls enforced
Description: Ensure processes enforce correct sequence of operations and detect invalid transitions.
Instructions: #### Action Items1. Model workflows with sequencing constraints.2. Implement system-level enforcement (e.g., gating, state transitions).3. Log sequence violations.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Workflow diagrams and logic documentation.- Implementation screenshots of enforced sequences.- Logs or alerts capturing sequence violations.

Title: Automated backups enabled
Description: Automated backups are enabled for all high-risk data and critical systems.
Instructions: ### Action itemsEstablish a system for automated backups on all high-risk data and critical systems. Ensure that this system is secure and that it backs up data on a regular basis, according to your organization's needs.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Oneleet monitors passing for all supported integrations, with automated backups enabled on all critical systems.2. Screenshots of backup configuration settings enabled on every critical system.
Frameworks: cfr_21_part_11_v1, cis_ig1_v1, eu_dora_v1, gdpr_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1, iso27001_v1, nist_800_171_v1, oneleet_essentials_v1, oneleet_pro_v1, pci_dss_level_1_v1, soc2_v1

Title: Business continuity and disaster recovery plan documented
Description: Document clear and practical business continuity and disaster recovery plan that outline procedures for maintaining essential operations during disruptions.
Instructions: **Action Items**Create an overarching business continuity and disaster recovery plan for your critical business systems or services, including:- Recovery time goals (e.g., "Customer database must be back online within 8 business hours").- Acceptable data loss limits where applicable (e.g., "Accounting data must not lose more than 1 day of entries").- Step-by-step recovery or continuity procedures.- List of responsible team members and their contact information.Review and update the plan after any significant changes to the IT infrastructure or business processes. Keep a log of test results and any issues encountered.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Documented business continuity and disaster recovery plan. Reach out to the Oneleet team for a template if needed.2. Emails or meeting notes showing the plan was recently reviewed and approved by management.

Title: Business continuity and disaster recovery policy established
Description: A comprehensive business continuity and disaster recovery policy is established, outlining the organization's strategies for responding to disruptive incidents and supporting business continuity.
Instructions: ### Action itemsDocument a business continuity and disaster recovery policy that outlines how your organization will respond to disruptive events, how you will promote the continuity of operations, and how you will recover in the event of a disaster.Review and update this policy regularly to account for changes in your organization.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Business continuity and disaster recovery policy uploaded to the Oneleet policy builder.2. PDF upload of the business continuity and disaster recovery policy with a dated version history.
Frameworks: eu_dora_v1, gdpr_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1, iso27001_v1, pci_dss_level_1_v1, soc2_v1

Title: Business continuity plans documented
Description: Document clear and practical business continuity plans that outline procedures for maintaining essential operations during disruptions.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsCreate separate, concise continuity plans for your critical business systems or services. For each plan, include:- Recovery time goals (e.g., "Customer database must be back online within 8 business hours").- Acceptable data loss limits where applicable (e.g., "Accounting data must not lose more than 1 day of entries").- Step-by-step recovery or continuity procedures.- List of responsible team members and their contact information.Implement a testing schedule for the continuity plans. Review and update the plan after any significant changes to the IT infrastructure or business processes. Keep a log of test results and any issues encountered.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Individual continuity plan documents for each critical system, including recovery steps and a list of team members responsible.2. Emails or meeting notes showing the plan was recently reviewed and approved by management.3. A master document listing all continuity plans with their last review and test dates.

Title: Business continuity plans tested
Description: Regularly test your business continuity plans to confirm their effectiveness and readiness to maintain critical operations during disruptions.
Instructions: **Action items**Schedule regular tests (at least annually) to evaluate your business continuity plan’s effectiveness and alignment with organizational goals. Use tabletop exercises or live simulations to test response capabilities for various disruption scenarios, involving relevant personnel across departments.Record outcomes, identify areas needing improvement, and implement corrective actions to strengthen the plan.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Documentation of test scenarios completed, list of attendees, date of testing, and frequency of testing.2. Summarized test results showing identified improvements and assigned corrective actions.3. Follow-up showing that corrective actions identified were subsequently completed.

Title: Business impact analysis performed
Description: To identify and prioritize critical business functions, assess potential impacts of disruptions, and determine recovery strategies through systematic business impact analysis (BIA).
Instructions: ### Action ItemsImplement a formal BIA process to identify critical business processes, systems, and dependencies across organizational units. Document maximum tolerable periods of disruption (MTPD), recovery time objectives (RTO), and recovery point objectives (RPO) for each critical process. Map dependencies between business processes, IT systems, and third-party services. Review and update the BIA annually and after major organizational changes. Present BIA results to stakeholders for incorporation into business continuity strategies.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Documented BIA methodology and procedures.- RTO and RPO documentation for critical processes.

Title: Crisis plan prepared
Description: Develop a crisis plan to facilitate a coordinated and effective response to security or operational incidents that may impact critical systems.
Instructions: **Action items**In your crisis plan, document the following items1. Develop a crisis management framework: Outline a structured approach that defines the scope, objectives, and guiding principles for crisis management. Identify the types of incidents that trigger the plan and specify the primary and secondary goals, such as maintaining operations, safeguarding data, and communicating with stakeholders.2. Define roles and responsibilities: Assign roles to crisis management team members, including incident commanders, communication leads, and technical support personnel. 3. Establish communication protocols: Create templates for various communication scenarios and designate preferred channels (e.g., email, SMS, press releases) to promote effective dissemination of information.Schedule periodic reviews to ensure that your crisis management plan remains relevant, incorporating lessons learned from past incidents, feedback from testing exercises, and updates in regulatory requirements. ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- A documented crisis management plan, outlining the framework, assigned roles, and response procedures.- Copies of communication templates for various incident types and records of approved communication channels.- Documentation of recent reviews and updates to the crisis management plan.

Title: Crisis plan tested
Description: Regularly test your crisis plan to validate its effectiveness and ensure all team members are prepared to respond swiftly and efficiently to potential disruptions. Define a testing schedule based on your organization’s risk profile and regulatory requirements. Conduct tests at least annually, or more frequently for high-risk scenarios, to promote ongoing preparedness.You can use a variety of testing methods, such as tabletop exercises, live simulations, and role-playing scenarios. Include all necessary stakeholders in testing exercises.Record outcomes from each test, noting any gaps, response delays, or areas requiring improvement. Document lessons learned, recommended changes, and action items to refine the crisis plan based on test results.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Documentation of testing schedules, test types, and the frequency of crisis plan tests.- Records of test outcomes, including detailed reports on findings, lessons learned, and action items.- Documentation of corrective actions taken in response to test results, with tracking records showing completion.- Records of follow-up tests to validate the effectiveness of corrective actions.

Title: Data backup and recovery policy established
Description: Develop and maintain a backup and recovery policy that defines the your approach to protecting and preserving critical data.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsCreate a backup policy document that outlines the scope, objectives, and key principles for data backup and recovery. Include sections on backup frequency, retention periods, storage locations, and types of backups (e.g., full, incremental, differential). Establish a process for regular review and update of the backup policy to reflect changes in technology, business requirements, and regulatory standards. Develop procedures for communicating the policy to relevant stakeholders and incorporating it into IT operations and disaster recovery planning.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Backup and Recovery Policy added to the Oneleet policy builder.2. Upload a Backup Policy with a dated version history and show evidence of employee acknowledgement.
Frameworks: eu_dora_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1, iso27001_v1, nist_800_171_v1

Title: Data recovery plan established
Description: Establish a data recovery plan that defines procedures for recovering data in case of data loss, corruption, or system failures. A robust data recovery plan helps minimize downtime and data loss in critical situations.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsDefine a data recovery plan that outlines the steps to be taken in case of data loss or corruption. This process should include information such as who to contact, what backup sources to use, and how to restore the data.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Data Recovery Plan added to the Oneleet policy builder.2. A screenshot or excerpt of your data recovery plan showing restoration procedures and notification steps

Title: Data recovery process established
Description: Establish a data recovery process that defines procedures for recovering data in case of data loss, corruption, or system failures. A robust data recovery process helps minimize downtime and data loss in critical situations.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsDefine a data recovery process that outlines the steps to be taken in case of data loss or corruption. This process should include information such as who to contact, what backup sources to use, and how to restore the data.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Data recovery policy uploaded to the Oneleet policy builder, defining the data recovery process.2. PDF upload of your data recovery process with a dated version history.
Frameworks: cfr_21_part_11_v1, cis_ig1_v1, gdpr_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1, iso27001_v1, pci_dss_level_1_v1, soc2_v1

Title: Data recovery tested
Description: Regularly test the data recovery process to validate its effectiveness and ensure that data can be successfully restored from backups. Testing helps identify any issues or gaps in the data recovery plan.
Instructions: ### Action itemsTesting the data recovery process should be a routine part of your organization's procedures. This could involve conducting monthly or quarterly tests to restore data from a backup and see if it's usable. Document the results of each test, including any issues encountered and how they were resolved.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. A document detailing the data recovery process and the schedule for testing it.2. Dated reports from recent data recovery tests.3. Timestamped screenshots from your backup system showing successful data restoration.
Frameworks: cfr_21_part_11_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1, iso27001_v1

Title: Digital operational resilience strategy created
Description: Establish and maintain a digital operational resilience strategy that outlines how your organization will ensure continuity, reliability, and security of critical ICT systems. This strategy should align with your business objectives and comply with regulatory standards focusing on preparedness, response, recovery, and adaptation to ICT-related disruptions.
Instructions: **Action items**Outline clear objectives that align with your mission, regulatory requirements, and risk tolerance. Define the scope to include all critical ICT systems, processes, and third-party dependencies.Establish a routine review process (e.g., annually or after significant changes in the threat landscape) to ensure the strategy remains current and responsive to emerging risks and regulatory updates.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- A documented digital operational resilience strategy stored in a secure repository.- Documentation of regular reviews and any updates to the strategy.

Title: Disaster recovery plans documented
Description: Document disaster recovery (DR) plans to recover critical systems and maintain operational continuity following disruptive incidents. These plans should address potential risks, define recovery strategies, and integrate with broader business resilience efforts.
Instructions: **Action Items**Identify and document key disaster scenarios, including cyberattacks, system failures, and natural disasters, and tailor specific recovery strategies for each situation. Develop disaster recovery plans that outline recovery objectives as well as step-by-step procedures, roles, and responsibilities for restoring critical systems and data for each scenario. Assign a dedicated disaster recovery coordinator and a cross-functional team to manage and execute the plan when needed, while also maintaining ownership of ongoing updates. Align the disaster recovery plan with the organization's overall business continuity strategy to provide a cohesive approach to handling disruptions. Store the plan securely, both digitally and physically, and make it accessible to authorized personnel who may need it during an emergency.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Documented disaster recovery plans with objectives, scenarios, recovery steps, assigned roles, and a date of last review.2. Records of recovery plan review and approval by management.
Frameworks: eu_dora_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1

Title: Disaster recovery plans tested
Description: Regularly test the organization's disaster recovery plans to ensure their effectiveness and identify areas for improvement. Testing helps validate the ability to recover critical systems and operations in the event of a disaster.
Instructions: ### Action itemsSchedule regular testing of your disaster recovery plan. This can be done on a semi-annual or annual basis, depending on the complexity and criticality of your organization's systems. All relevant personnel should be involved in the testing process to ensure a comprehensive evaluation.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Disaster recovery test plans and results documentation showing when the tests were performed, who was involved, what findings were identified, and how those issues are being addressed.
Frameworks: eu_dora_v1, gdpr_v1, hipaa_business_associate_no_phi_v1, hipaa_business_associate_v1, hipaa_clearinghouse_v1, hipaa_health_plan_v1, hipaa_provider_v1, hipaa_v1, iso27001_v1, oneleet_essentials_v1, oneleet_pro_v1, pci_dss_level_1_v1, soc2_v1

Title: Recovery data isolated
Description: Isolate the recovery data from the production environment to prevent accidental overwriting or corruption of backups. Keeping recovery data separate helps maintain the integrity and availability of backup copies.
Instructions: ### Action ItemsEnsure that your recovery data is stored separately from your production environment. This could be a different server, a cloud storage bucket, or a physically separate data center.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Screenshots or links to your backup configuration settings showing recovery data is stored separately from the production environment.2. Documentation from your cloud provider outlining backup configuration settings for your database, demonstrating backups are isolated from production databases.

Title: DMARC policy and verification used
Description: DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance) policy and verification mechanisms are implemented to prevent email spoofing and phishing attacks.
Instructions: ### Action itemsFor a successful DMARC implementation, it is imperative to first establish a DMARC policy within your DNS records. Prior to activating DMARC, ensure that both DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) and Sender Policy Framework (SPF) configurations are in place and have been actively authenticating messages for a minimum duration of 48 hours. This preparatory step is crucial for the seamless integration and effectiveness of DMARC.### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control1. Oneleet monitor passing for all in-scope domains.2. Screenshots or copy of your published DMARC DNS records for all in-scope domains.3. Screenshots of DMARC reports showing that checks are being performed and acted upon for all in-scope domains.**Important**: In the event that your DNS provider indicates an issue regarding the length of the DKIM TXT value, it is permissible to segment the TXT value into smaller portions. Each segment should be enclosed in double quotes, with a space separating consecutive segments. This method is generally effective in resolving such issues.

Title: Point-of-Interaction devices managed
Description: Implement procedures to securely manage all Point-of-Interaction (POI) devices (e.g., card payment devices) throughout their lifecycle, from deployment to decommissioning.To keep your payment devices safe:- Know your devices: Make an inventory of all your payment devices, including where they are located. Review this list every few months to make sure it's up to date.- Lock them down: Attach devices securely to counters so they can't be easily stolen and put tamper-evident stickers on device openings.- Check devices regularly: Look at each device daily for anything odd (like new attachments). Tell your staff to report anything suspicious right away.- Control who touches the devices: Only let authorized people install or fix devices. Always watch repair technicians when they work on devices.- Use strong passwords: Change the default password on every device and use unique, hard-to-guess passwords.- Train your team: Show everyone how to spot a tampered device and train them what to do if they see something suspicious.- Be careful when getting rid of old devices: Wipe all data from devices before throwing them away. Update your inventory accordingly.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- Your list of payment devices- Photos of secured devices- Records of staff training- Logs of device repairs or replacements

Title: Unexpected file changes monitored
Description: Set up a system to monitor unexpected changes to your important files and set up alerts when they happen.Create a list of your most important files. This should include things like system files, application files, configuration files, and log files. Review and update this list on a regular basis.Set up a file monitoring tool or intrusion detection tool with file monitoring capabilities. It should check all the important files on your list at least once a week. Configure it to send alerts immediately if it spots any unexpected changes. This could be changes to the file's content, or if files are added or deleted when they shouldn't be.Decide who on your team should receive these alerts and make sure they know what to do when they get one. Write down a clear procedure for how to investigate and respond to unexpected file changes. Keep records of all the alerts you receive and how you responded to them.Finally, check your file monitoring system at least once a month to make sure it's working as intended.
Instructions: ### Examples of evidence that satisfy this control- List of critical files being monitored- Configuration settings for your file integrity monitoring tool- Logs showing weekly file checks- Records of alerts from your file monitoring system and how you responded- Written procedure for responding to file change alerts- Logs of your regular tests of the file monitoring system

Title: Infrastructure changes logged

Screenshots of your Infrastructure as Code (IaC) branch commit history. Screenshots must display a visible system date/time.2. Title: Systems segregated according to sensitivity of business function

Evaluate which functions need higher security and which need less. Separate them where possible (e.g., use different servers or cloud services for operations with different security needs). If you can't physically separate use virtualization to create separate environments on the same machine.Secure everything to the level of the most sensitive function.Document your setup by creating a diagram showing which business operations are on which systems. Explain how you've separated or secured different operations.

Title: Compliance monitoring processes defined and implemented

Title: Critical security control systems monitored
Description: Your security tools are like the alarm system for your business. If they stop working and you don't notice, you're leaving the door wide open for attackers. By catching and fixing problems fast, you keep your defenses strong and your customer data safe.Make a list of your key security tools, including:- Firewalls and other network security controls- Intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS)- Anti-malware software- Systems that detect changes to important files- Physical access controls (like door locks)- Login systems- Tools that record system activities (audit logs)- Any tools that separate different parts of your network (segmentation controls)Configure each system to send out an alert if it stops working properly and make sure these alerts go to the right people who can fix the problem. Include this in the incident response plan for each type of security system.Regularly test your alert system to make sure it's working and maintain records for each security system failure.

Title: Digital operational resilience testing program defined
Description: Define a comprehensive testing program to evaluate your digital operational resilience across ICT systems and critical functions. Set objectives for testing key resilience aspects such as incident response, disaster recovery, and business continuity.Determine appropriate testing methods, including tabletop exercises, penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and full-scale simulations. Schedule tests regularly, ensuring they reflect the criticality and risk profile of each asset or service.Analyze testing outcomes to identify areas for improvement and update security and resilience controls accordingly. Document action plans to address any identified gaps and implement corrective actions.

Title: HHS breach reporting template established

Title: Incident response policy established

Establish criteria for incidents requiring formal review, including any events affecting critical functions. Define the scope to include root cause analysis, impact assessment, response effectiveness, and corrective actions.For each qualifying incident, hold a review meeting promptly after resolution. Ensure that relevant stakeholders participate. Record detailed findings from each review, including root causes, identified vulnerabilities, and gaps in response. Document action items, timelines, and responsible parties for implementing corrective measures to prevent recurrence.Establish a follow-up process to verify that corrective actions are completed and effective.

Title: Information security during disruption

Title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation
Description: The organization shall determine:  a) what needs to be monitored and measured, including information security processes and controls;   b) the methods for monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation, as applicable, to ensure valid results. The methods selected should produce comparable and reproducible results to be considered valid;   c) when the monitoring and measuring shall be performed;   d) who shall monitor and measure;   e) when the results from monitoring and measurement shall be analyzed and evaluated;   f) who shall analyze and evaluate these results.   Documented information shall be available as evidence of the results.   The organization shall evaluate the information security performance and the effectiveness of the information security management system.

Title: Network infrastructure monitored
Description: Implement monitoring mechanisms for the network infrastructure to detect and respond to suspicious or unauthorized activities. Network monitoring helps ensure the integrity and availability of network resources.

Title: Transaction security and authentication safeguarded

Title: Unauthorized changes to payment pages managed
Description: Implement a change and tamper detection mechanism for your payment pages. This system should monitor two key elements: the HTTP headers (which contain metadata about the web page) and the actual content of the page, with particular attention to any scripts running on it. Configure your detection mechanism to identify any unauthorized modifications, additions, or deletions to these components of your payment pages.Determine the frequency of your page checks. You have two options:- Perform checks at least once a week (recommended for most businesses), or- Establish your own schedule based on a thorough risk analysis (if you're unsure about this process, weekly checks are advisable).Your detection system should promptly alert your team to any suspicious activity. Designate responsible personnel to receive these alerts and establish clear procedures for how to respond.If you're utilizing a payment Vendor that embeds their payment form on your website, request evidence that they're performing these checks on their end. They should be able to demonstrate compliance with this requirement.Regularly review and update your detection mechanism. As your payment pages evolve or new threats emerge, you may need to adjust your monitoring parameters.Maintain detailed records of all alerts received and your responses to them. This documentation aids in incident analysis and demonstrates your vigilance to auditors.

Title: AI human oversight established

Description: Establish and maintain an AI policy that defines the organization's approach to responsible development, deployment, and use of artificial intelligence systems.

Description: Maintain comprehensive technical documentation for AI systems, including architecture, capabilities, limitations, and information for users and stakeholders.

Title: Board expertise developed
Description: Ensure that the Board of Directors possesses the expertise and skills necessary to provide effective oversight and guidance on various business and industry matters.

Title: Business associate agreements managed

These agreements must extend your obligations under BAAs with covered entities or upstream business associates, including safeguarding PHI, reporting breaches, and adhering to HIPAA standards.

Title: Capacity management
Description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

Title: Change management

Title: Change management policy established
Description: Establish a change management policy that defines procedures for controlling and documenting changes to systems, applications, and infrastructure.

Title: Change management process established
